title: File Servers, Object Storage, CDNs, and Architecture Selection · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs file servers, object storage, cdns, and architecture selection from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for architecture and responsibility decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.
Choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 4485 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

Inspect the Cost Failure Model trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Cost Failure Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Cost Failure Model, keep Cost Failure Model connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Cost Failure Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Cost Failure Model happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Cost Failure Model, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Cost Failure Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Cost Failure Model, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 7914 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Cost Failure Model, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: S3 storage price alone determines the cheapest architecture.
